regions.us-ports: Today at Miami I am seeing SSE 8kt winds with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, and I am coordinating gangway and lightning-sensitive shore excursion timing with the terminal. Over the next 24 hours Miami and Port Everglades are forecast SSE 11kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability for Day 1, while 24-48 hours out Port Canaveral and Tampa Bay are forecast SSW 11kt with thunderstorms and 22% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 brings SE 11kt with thunderstorms and 17% rain probability, so vessels and excursion teams should remain aware of persistent thunderstorm sky conditions. I will update passengers and crew as I monitor the current SSE 8kt winds, the SSE 11kt Day 1 trend, and the 22% rain probability on Day 2 at Port Canaveral to keep embarkation and shore plans aligned with lightning and wind conditions.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today I am issuing a priority weather summary for our Bahamas and Central Caribbean calls: thunderstorms with less than 10% rain probability today affecting Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, Key West, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná, thunderstorms with less than 10% rain probability on day 2, and thunderstorms with 19% rain probability on day 3. For operations today I see SE 5kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability in Nassau, so I am authorizing routine embarkation and disembarkation procedures at Nassau and advising shore excursion teams in Freeport and Bimini to proceed with standard tendering under SE 5kt and clear skies. Over the next 24 to 48 hours the Nassau forecast shows SE 10kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability on day 1 and SE 7kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability on day 2, and I will monitor Key West, the Berry Islands, and Grand Cayman for increased convective clouds and adjust outdoor excursion timing based on SE 10kt and SE 7kt winds. Looking beyond 48 hours Day 3 in Nassau calls for SE 12kt with thunderstorms and 19% rain probability, and I am directing ship operations in Ocho Rios, Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná to track the SE 12kt wind and the 19% rain probability while maintaining flexible shore excursion schedules.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today: PRIORITY ALERT, significant weather today: thunderstorms, 12% rain probability; significant weather day 3: thunderstorms, 35% rain probability; ports most affected are San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix. I am monitoring current E 10kt with clear skies and a 12% rain probability in San Juan while the daytime forecast shows E 17kt with thunderstorms and a 12% rain probability, so embarkation and gangway operations in San Juan and St. Thomas are continuing under E 10kt clear skies with crews prepared for stronger E 17kt winds later. Over the next 24 to 48 hours I expect E 18kt and partly cloudy conditions with less than 10% rain probability for San Juan and nearby ports such as St. Maarten and Antigua, and I will watch E 18kt as the primary factor for shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours the forecast shifts to E 16kt with thunderstorms and a 35% rain probability on day 3, which may affect tender operations and shore excursions in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, I’m issuing a priority alert: active thunderstorms with 22% rain probability are present for Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City, and Cozumel is currently reporting SE 11 kt winds and thunderstorms. For today’s port operations and shore excursions in Cozumel and Costa Maya I am tracking SE 13 kt winds with thunderstorms and a 22% rain probability, and I will delay or modify tendering and exposed-deck activities if thunderstorm cells move over the harbor under those SE 13 kt conditions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Roatan and Belize City are forecast with ESE 9 kt winds and thunderstorms with less than 10% rain probability, and with ESE 9 kt I expect calmer seas for docking while maintaining a thunderstorm watch. Beyond 48 hours the outlook shows ESE 16 kt winds with thunderstorms and 26% rain probability for Cozumel and Costa Maya, so I am planning for increased ESE 16 kt winds during maneuvering and gangway operations while we continue to monitor thunderstorm trends.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today I am issuing a priority alert: thunderstorms with 36% rain probability and elevated E 20kt winds forecast for today may affect port operations and shore excursions in Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena; current Aruba conditions are E 15kt, overcast, 36% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours Aruba remains forecast E 19kt with thunderstorms and 16% rain probability, so I will continue to monitor E 19kt east winds for operational impacts at Cartagena and across our Southern Caribbean itinerary.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today significant weather: thunderstorms, 25% rain probability; Day 2: thunderstorms, 87% rain probability; Day 3: thunderstorms, 60% rain probability, with Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada the ports most affected.
